skill.name: open-targets-db
skill.description: Query the Open Targets Platform to retrieve targets, diseases, or evidence records when you need target-disease association data and evidence-based scores for therapeutic discovery.
skill.summary: Run queries against the Open Targets Platform to find therapeutic target-disease associations with evidence-based scoring.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 57 static analysis findings were evaluated and classified as false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are false matches on Python string formatting (%) and normal markdown text, not actual cryptography. The 'shell backtick execution' findings are standard markdown code formatting backticks within documentation blocks, not command execution. The 'system reconnaissance' findings match normal variable names and comments in code and documentation. Hardcoded URLs and HTTP requests point to the legitimate Open Targets Platform public API. One minor code quality observation: the search query on line 67 of query_opentargets.py uses string interpolation instead of parameterized variables. The skill makes legitimate HTTP requests to the Open Targets Platform public API for its declared purpose of therapeutic target discovery. Overall risk is low and the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: String interpolation in GraphQL query (GraphQL injection risk)
low_findings.description: The search query type on line 67 uses Python string formatting (%) to insert entity_id directly into a GraphQL query string instead of using parameterized variables. While the target is a read-only public API, this is a code quality concern that could enable unintended queries with crafted input.

content.user_title: Query Open Targets for target-disease associations
content.value_statement: Drug researchers need to find and prioritize therapeutic targets linked to diseases using structured biomedical data. This skill queries the Open Targets Platform to retrieve targets, diseases, and evidence records with curated association scores.
actual_capabilities: Query targets by Ensembl gene ID to retrieve associated diseases and evidence scores
Query diseases by EFO ontology ID to discover associated therapeutic targets
Perform free-text search across the Open Targets database
Access evidence from integrated sources including GWAS Catalog, ClinVar, and ChEMBL
Retrieve association scores based on Open Targets evidence aggregation
limitations: Requires internet access to reach the Open Targets Platform API
Only supports the entity types: target, disease, and search
Does not provide clinical recommendations or medical advice
Result quality depends on the accuracy of the identifier provided

use_cases.title: Drug target discovery for a disease
use_cases.description: A researcher inputs an EFO disease ID to discover and prioritize candidate therapeutic targets ranked by association score.

use_cases.title: Target-to-disease association review
use_cases.description: A biologist inputs an Ensembl gene ID to review all diseases associated with a target and the supporting evidence sources.

use_cases.title: Evidence aggregation for pipeline integration
use_cases.description: A bioinformatics scientist programmatically fetches curated target-disease associations for downstream analysis in a research pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Basic target query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the open-targets-db skill to query target ENSG00000157764 and list its associated diseases with scores.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user exploring a single gene target

prompt_templates.title: Disease target discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the open-targets-db skill to query disease EFO_0000270 and find the top 10 associated therapeutic targets.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user researching a specific disease

prompt_templates.title: Free-text search across entities
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the open-targets-db skill to search for 'BRCA1' across all entity types and summarize the results.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative target analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the open-targets-db skill to query targets ENSG00000157764 and ENSG00000141510. Compare their disease associations and evidence scores side by side.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user comparing multiple therapeutic targets

output_examples.input: Query target ENSG00000157764 (BRAF) and show its associated diseases.
output_examples.output:
Target: BRAF (ENSG00000157764)
Approved name: B-Raf proto-oncogene, serine/threonine kinase
Associated diseases found: 12
Top associations by score:
- Malignant melanoma (score: 0.95)
- Colorectal cancer (score: 0.72)
- Thyroid cancer (score: 0.68)
- Non-small cell lung carcinoma (score: 0.55)
Evidence sources: GWAS Catalog, ClinVar, ChEMBL

output_examples.input: Query disease EFO_0000270 (asthma) and show associated therapeutic targets.
output_examples.output:
Disease: Asthma (EFO_0000270)
Associated targets found: 85
Top targets by association score:
- IL4R (score: 0.88) - Interleukin 4 receptor
- IL13 (score: 0.82) - Interleukin 13
- TSLP (score: 0.76) - Thymic stromal lymphopoietin
- FCER2 (score: 0.65) - Fc fragment of IgE receptor II
Evidence aggregation: harmonic-sum across genetics, drugs, pathways, and literature

best_practices: Use precise Ensembl gene IDs or EFO ontology IDs for the most accurate query results instead of free-text search.
Validate API connectivity before running large batch queries by testing a single known identifier first.
Cross-reference high-scoring associations with primary literature before drawing research conclusions.
anti_patterns: Using free-text search when an exact ontology identifier is available, which reduces result precision.
Interpreting Open Targets association scores as direct clinical or treatment recommendations.
Making medical treatment decisions based solely on computational association data without human expert review.

faq.question: What is the Open Targets Platform?
faq.answer: The Open Targets Platform is a public resource that integrates biomedical data from genetics, drugs, and literature to support systematic target-disease association analysis for drug discovery.

faq.question: What identifier formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill accepts Ensembl gene IDs for targets (e.g., ENSG00000157764) and EFO ontology IDs for diseases (e.g., EFO_0000270). Free-text search terms are also supported.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without internet access?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires an active internet connection to send GraphQL queries to the Open Targets Platform API.

faq.question: What do the association scores mean?
faq.answer: Association scores range from 0 to 1 and represent the strength of evidence linking a target to a disease. Higher scores indicate stronger support from multiple data sources.

faq.question: How is the evidence aggregated?
faq.answer: Open Targets aggregates evidence using harmonic-sum style aggregation across sources such as GWAS, ClinVar, ChEMBL, pathways, and literature to prevent any single data type from dominating.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for clinical decision-making?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides research-level association data only. It is not validated for clinical use and should not be used to make treatment decisions.
